Total duration of sensor recording session
Stride-adjusted distance at which the audio beep fired. Computed at trial start as max(1.5, 3.0 - 0.8 * gait_speed). Varies per participant.

Field Note: How the trial ended - QC marker
Clinical TUG time: start cue to detected sit-down stillness. Thresholds: <10s normal, 10-20s variable, >=13.5s fall-risk.
Soft proxy for stand-up duration: time from go cue to first detected step.
Step count during the walk-out segment (first step to cue beep).
Estimated distance during walk-out segment. Beep fires at the stride-adjusted target (see tug_cue_distance_m), not always at exactly 3.0 m.
Duration of walk-out segment (first detected step to cue beep).
Mean stride length over walk-out segment (Weinberg estimator). First stride excluded.
Coefficient of variation of stride length (SD / mean). Higher = more irregular gait.
Steps per minute over walk-out segment.
Mean inter-step interval over walk-out segment.
Coefficient of variation of inter-step interval. Gait variability marker.
Walk-out distance / duration. Clinical cutoff <0.8 m/s suggests mobility impairment.
ISO 8601 UTC. Snapshotted from UserProfile.tug_step_calibration so each TUG row knows which calibration produced its template.
Total accepted W-windows that contributed to the mean template during calibration.
Number of 5-step calibration batches walked (target 4 - range 2-6).
Runtime detection threshold. Sliding-window template correlation must exceed this to count as a step. Equals 0.85 * median(per-window calibration correlations).
Mean Weinberg stride length learned during calibration. Used by the cue-distance formula.
Mean inter-step interval observed during calibration. Used by the cue-distance formula.
Template L2 change between the last two calibration batches (lower = more stable). Blank if only one batch was collected.
Per-batch detection rate (accepted / 5 expected) pipe-joined, e.g. 0.80|0.60|0.80|1.00. Trend going up across batches suggests gait stabilising.
App version when the calibration was created. Distinct from tug_app_version (the version that ran this test).

Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, 1 - Very well | 2, 2 - Better than usual | 3, 3 - Neutral | 4, 4 - More fatigued than usual | 5, 5 - Very fatigued
